mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.50%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, it signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which forces equity markets to price in imminent recessionary losses, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE crosses above 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery. This joint strength in output and residential investment drives corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when GDPC1 and HOUST both trend upward, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum. In a low-inflation environment, this supply expansion translates to higher corporate margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or loses significance when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, or 5-day forward returns show negative correlation with INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 220.0, it triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening. Anticipated rate hikes compress equity multiples and increase borrowing costs, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates robust labor market expansion. This steady job creation supports consumer confidence and spending, which directly fuels corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple 3-year rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real single-family housing starts and existing home sales confirms sustained residential demand. This housing market strength drives construction activity and related supply chains, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined housing signal fails to predict positive returns when both series trend upward, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and rising unemployment indicates structural supply inefficiency and labor market slack. This mismatch signals weakening economic fundamentals, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts positive returns instead of negative, or loses predictive power when the economy enters a clear recessionary phase.
